title: Satin Ethiopian Eggplant Tibs
description: A jammy, creamy, monsoon-inspired Ethiopian tibs made with tender eggplant and a rich coconut-based sauce.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat and sauté the onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced eggplant to the skillet and cook, stirring frequently, until the pieces begin to soften, about 7 minutes.
Stir in the tomato paste and fresh tomatoes, cooking until the tomatoes break down into a thick sauce, about 4 minutes.
Sprinkle the berbere, cumin, and cardamom over the mixture, mixing well to coat the vegetables evenly, then pour in the coconut milk.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the dish simmer gently for 15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the eggplant to become tender.
In a small bowl, whisk the chickpea flour with a few tablespoons of the hot cooking liquid to make a smooth slurry, then stir it into the skillet to thicken the sauce.
Continue to simmer, uncovered, for an additional 5 minutes until the sauce reaches a silky, jammy consistency, stirring occasionally.
Taste and adjust seasoning if desired, then serve the satin, creamy tibs hot over injera or cooked rice.
